title: Continuity prompt + objective ledger (working-memory MVP)
description: Highest-impact-first fix for the flailing. (1) Rewrite AUTONOMY_PROMPT for CONTINUITY: stop telling it to re-scan/re-plan each tick; tell it to continue its current objective, finish before starting anything new, do not re-scan known areas. (2) Persisted per-agent objective ledger {objective, plan_steps, progress_notes} next to the session files. (3) set_objective/update_plan mechanism (MCP tool or structured trailer) so the agent COMMITS to and updates its own goal. (4) Inject ledger + one-line live state into each autonomy tick. Goals stay self-chosen; we add follow-through. companion/bridge/pipe.py + maybe one MCP tool.

title: Continuity prompt + objective ledger (working-memory MVP)
description: Highest-impact-first fix for the flailing. title: Factorio-competent agent harness (executive function: curriculum + skills + memory + plan/execute)
description:
PROBLEM. The autonomy loop gives the agent a heartbeat (motivation) but no executive function. Observed live: it bounces between objectives, never finishes anything (disassembled the spaceship, then ran around re-scanning and re-planning every tick). Root causes: (1) the autonomy prompt RESETS intent each tick ("check your situation, pick something"); (2) no persistent goal/plan/memory — continuity lives only in a noisy transcript; (3) no compact world-state injection so it burns turns re-scanning; (4) no commitment device (never "finish before switching"); (5) open-ended early game with no chosen objective = flail. GLM's weaker long-horizon planning amplifies all of this.

GOAL. Build the agent-side executive layer so the AI is actually decent at playing Factorio: forms and COMMITS to objectives, plans, remembers, reuses skills, verifies outcomes, and makes coherent long-horizon progress — while keeping goals self-directed ("it does its own thing").

PRIOR ART (researched via exa):
- Voyager (NVIDIA, TMLR'24, arxiv 2305.16291) — the template: (a) automatic curriculum that proposes the next achievable task, (b) a SKILL LIBRARY of stored, named, composable executable skills that grows capability, (c) iterative prompting = self-verification + env feedback + error correction.
- Factorio Learning Environment / FLE (Hopkins/Bakler/Khan, NeurIPS'25, github.com/JackHopkins/factorio-learning-environment, arxiv 2503.09617) — purpose-built Factorio-LLM benchmark for long-term planning + program synthesis + automation. Both a design goldmine AND a yardstick. Study their API abstraction (they use Python program synthesis, not raw RCON) and their eval task suite.
- Memory literature: Generative Agents (memory stream + reflection); SYNAPSE (episodic+semantic); GAM (hierarchical graph memory); reflective memory management; "memory is reconstructed not retrieved" (graph memory).
- Planning: Plan-and-Act (ICML'25), ADaPT (as-needed decomposition), ReAcTree (hierarchical agent trees), RECAP (recursive context-aware planning), Devil's Advocate (anticipatory reflection).

MEMORY DESIGN (ponder -> recommendation). Factorio state is HIGHLY STRUCTURED, so prefer structured/queryable memory over free-text retrieval (matches this project's "offloading" philosophy: hand the LLM structured state, don't make it re-read a transcript or do similarity search). Recommended HYBRID across four memory types:
- Working memory: current {objective, plan_steps, progress} — small, always injected, agent-updated. (The objective ledger; highest-impact-first.)
- Semantic/world memory: base + map knowledge (what is built where, resource patches, zones). PARTIALLY ALREADY OFFLOADED to the engine (zones, scan_resources, protected resources, blueprints) — extend with a queryable "base index / situation report".
- Procedural memory: a Voyager-style skill library (named, parameterized, reusable build macros) — the capability multiplier.
- Episodic memory: an append-only journal of events/accomplishments/failures, compacted by periodic REFLECTION into lessons that feed the curriculum. Use free-text+reflection only for the genuinely narrative parts.
Defer vector/RAG and full graph memory until scale demands them; structured state + reflective journal + skill library is the right starting architecture.

SCOPE NOTE. This is mostly the COMPANION/bridge agent loop plus a few new MCP tools (compact situation_report, verify-production); the engine already does the spatial offloading (pathfinding, belt routing, zones, blueprints). Relates to upj.3 (trustworthy action feedback) for the self-verification piece.

Children decompose into: continuity prompt + objective ledger (MVP) -> situation report tool -> plan/execute split -> skill library -> episodic journal + reflection -> outcome self-verification -> study FLE -> eval/benchmark harness.
close_reason: All 8 children merged & closed: hfb (ledger), u42 (situation_report), 7u0 (plan/execute), q6u (FLE study), biw (verify_production), avo (journal+reflection), dzx (skill library), fea (eval harness). Executive-function harness complete: continuity + cheap orientation + plan/execute cadence + outcome verification + episodic memory + reusable skills + a scoreboard. main green (cargo + python).

title: Bot character stored in global.* instead of 2.0 storage.*

With a human online, place/craft/walk/mine/clear operate on the HUMAN body. Make lookup agent_id-scoped via storage.characters; dedupe into one helper.

Dedupe ~25 inline copies into one helper. Unifies Findings 2,3,4,8. Outcome: agent drives ITS body; human keeps theirs.

title: North star: LLM teammate that plays Factorio multiplayer alongside a human
description: Umbrella goal: an LLM agent that joins our multiplayer game as its OWN body (not puppeting the human), can chat in-game, take legible actions, and not fall over on a real-sized factory. Child features track the capability gaps; audit epic tracks the engine rot underneath.

title: Study Factorio Learning Environment (FLE) for harness and eval design
description: Research spike. Mine github.com/JackHopkins/factorio-learning-environment (NeurIPS25, arxiv 2503.09617): agent harness, API abstraction (Python program synthesis vs our RCON Lua: what is the right action surface), memory/curriculum approach, and especially the EVAL TASK SUITE. Decide what to adopt and whether to use their tasks as our benchmark. Output: short design note + recommendations for the other children.
close_reason: FLE study complete. Design note at .choir/context/fle-study-q6u.md. Key: FLE = Python program-synthesis REPL + typed tools + RecursiveReportFormatter memory (EXISTING STRUCTURES + ERROR TIPS, chunk=16). Models weakest at error recovery + spatial reasoning. Recommendations: avo mirrors their reflection shape; biw (verify/error-recovery) is highest leverage; dzx = named tool-call macros w/ injected signatures; fea adopts 'make 16 X/min' throughput milestones + production score. Stay tool-calls, not program-synthesis, for now.

title: Episodic journal + periodic reflection
description: Generative-Agents-style episodic memory. Append events (accomplishments, failures, discoveries) to a per-agent journal; periodic REFLECTION compacts it into durable lessons that feed the curriculum (north iron patch exhausted; inserter faced wrong way last time). Inject recent events + reflections, not raw transcript. companion/bridge.

title: Skill library (Voyager-style reusable build macros)
description: Capability multiplier (Voyager). Capture successful action sequences as NAMED, PARAMETERIZED, reusable skills (build_burner_mining_setup, lay_smelting_line, feed_lab). Store as procedural memory; retrieve relevant skills for the current objective; compose them. Avoids re-deriving common builds and grows competence over sessions. Study FLE's action API for how to express composable actions.

title: Plan/execute split (deliberative planner pass + cheap execution ticks)
description: Separate deliberation from execution (Plan-and-Act / ReAcTree / ADaPT). A less-frequent PLANNER pass reviews state + objective and sets/updates the concrete plan and the next curriculum milestone; frequent cheap EXECUTION ticks act against the stored plan without re-planning. Optionally a stronger model for the planner pass while GLM executes. Depends on the objective ledger (hfb). companion/bridge.

title: Wire claude-in-factorio bridge to spawn/drive named agents via factorioctl
description: factorioctl now gives named agents their own bodies + movement (upj.1), but the on_tick step-walk driver is RCON-injected and SERVER-SIDE only — with a connected human client it can desync. Deterministic MP movement belongs in the claude-in-factorio mod's own on_tick. Wire the bridge to: set FACTORIO_AGENT_ID to real names, spawn via factorioctl init_character, and drive movement deterministically. Makes the multi-agent companion path actually work end-to-end.
notes: DETERMINISTIC MP MOVEMENT LANDED (PR #10, tree cc3575e, 4-round audit clean). Walk loop now runs in the claude-interface mod's lockstep on_tick (process_walk_targets); factorioctl set_walk_target/clear/active route through the mod remote (register_character + set_walk_target + has_walk_target) with level-script fallback when the mod is absent; anti-double-drive clear + expires_tick safety; no-body errors 'spawn first' instead of queuing a doomed target. REMAINING: TL+human live MP smoke (connect Steam client, confirm the AI walks smoothly with no desync). Code-complete; pending live verification.
close_reason: Deterministic MP movement implementation merged + audited (4 rounds) last session: walk loop runs in the claude-interface mod on_tick (lockstep on all peers) via process_walk_targets; factorioctl set_walk_target/clear_walk_target/walk_target_active route through the mod remote with level-script fallback. Functionally complete; live MP smoke (human in Steam + AI body walking without desync) is the remaining real-world confirmation, not blocking the bead.

title: Add GitHub Actions CI (cargo test) so green-CI satisfies the merge verify gate
description: No CI on the fork meant verify_satisfied_by_ci=false forced per-push local TDD attestation, which deadlocks after a post-review fix push (terminal TDD phase, no honest re-attest). Add .github/workflows/ci.yml running cargo build/test on push+PR so future waves fork with verify_satisfied_by_ci=true and green CI satisfies the gate.

A quote/backslash/newline breaks syntax or runs arbitrary Lua over RCON. Add a proper Lua-literal escaper.

New format used correctly elsewhere (lua.rs:467,601,665,736,1921,...).

title: RCON reads a single packet, no ID/type validation
description: Finding 12: src/client/rcon.rs:115-169 returns the next packet body with no request_id/packet_type/terminator handling; unused SERVERDATA_* consts (rcon.rs:13-15). Source RCON fragments long responses -> truncated JSON -> random serde failures. Implement multi-packet reassembly.

title: Mutating actions swallow Lua error JSON and report success
description: Finding 7: src/client/mod.rs:666,701,642,359,367 discard Lua {"error":...} from insert_items/set_recipe/remove_entity/teleport/walk; MCP (mcp.rs:1285,1321,1337) and CLI announce success anyway. Propagate real outcomes.

title: extract_items deposits into game.players[1], not the acting character
description: Finding 8: src/client/lua.rs:1195-1203 hard-codes game.players[1] for the destination inventory; wrong in MP, fails headless ('No player character found'). MCP desc claims 'into character inventory' (mcp.rs:1292). Use the same agent-scoped character.

title: Entity-by-unit_number is a hardcoded +/-500 area scan
description: Finding 4: src/client/lua.rs:78,110,1068,1093,1134,1178,1241 implement get-entity-by-id as find_entities_filtered over a 1000x1000 box. Entities outside +/-500 are 'not found'; every insert/extract/remove/rotate/set_recipe is O(area). Use a real registry/reliable lookup.

title: Trustworthy action feedback (no false success)
description: A teammate must know when actions fail. Mutating ops swallow Lua error JSON and report success (Finding 7). Surface real outcomes via CLI + MCP.
close_reason: Action tools report honest, granular success/failure (no false success). Spot-checked: place_entity returns distinct errors for missing item / can_place fail / create_entity nil; insert_items returns ACTUAL inserted count and an explicit 'inserted 0 (full/not accepted)' error; craft returns success=crafted>0 with the real queued count from begin_crafting (respects ingredients). Established by upj.1 + cjf audit hardening; outcome-level verification added via biw (verify_production).

title: Robust RCON transport for large factories
description: Large Lua JSON responses fragment across packets; client reads one packet, no ID/type validation (Finding 12) -> random parse failures as the factory grows.
close_reason: Already resolved (clean-sweep engine hardening). rcon.rs receive() uses read_exact(size) to fully reassemble large length-prefixed responses (1MB cap); command_response_body validates request_id (skips stray packets) AND packet_type (bails on mismatch); execute() loops matching by id. Finding 12 (single-packet read, no id/type validation) is addressed; encode/decode tests present.

title: Reliable in-game chat loop
description: Agent must reliably receive player chat and reply. on_console_chat handler is registered from an RCON command with errors discarded (Finding 11).
close_reason: Reliable in-game chat loop shipped: register_chat_handler re-registers on_console_chat unconditionally (fixing the save/load stale-handler bug); verified live via Tier-3 headless save/reload regression. Leftover dead-field cleanup tracked as eyc.

title: ServerManager only tracks processes it started (status/stop theater)
description: Finding 14: src/client/server.rs:11-12 keeps server_process in memory; status/stop (server.rs:120-135) only see a child this instance spawned. A per-invocation CLI can't see a server started earlier; tests fall back to pgrep (tests/setup.sh:67). Track via pidfile/port probe.

title: Inline-Lua-comment footgun live and violated at mcp.rs:684
description: Finding 9: CLAUDE.md:372-384 forbids same-line trailing Lua comments because the executor (mod.rs:56-73) joins lines with spaces and only strips lines starting with --. src/bin/mcp.rs:684 has 'local belt_dir = dir  -- comment' which eats the rest of the joined line; can corrupt get_machine_belt_positions. Strip/forbid in code, not vibes.

title: MCP uses get_entity_by_unit_number that core says doesn't work
description: Finding 10: src/bin/mcp.rs:676-679 uses game.get_entity_by_unit_number for drills, but src/client/lua.rs:1132 explicitly notes it 'doesn't work for Lua-created entities' and uses a scan. The 'ALWAYS use this tool' path (mcp.rs:654) can fail for the very entities this engine creates.
